perturbation.original_text: 1. In the event that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital for each of the Company's first and third fiscal quarters (as determined based on the Company's first and third fiscal quarterly filed statutory financial statements, respectively, subject to any adjustments or modifications thereto required by the Domiciliary State's insurance department or the Company's independent auditors) falls below the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's projected Company Action Level RBC (in each case as estimated by the Company as of the end of each such first and third fiscal quarters, as the case may be), AIG shall, within the respective time periods set forth under paragraph 4, in accordance with paragraph 5 and in compliance with applicable law, provide to the Company cash, cash equivalents, securities or other instruments that qualify (as admitted assets) for purposes of calculating the Company's Total Adjusted Capital, as a contribution and not as a loan, in an amount such that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital as of the end of each of the Company's second and fourth fiscal quarter, as the case may be, will be projected to be at least equal to the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's Company Action Level RBC. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems necessary in its sole discretion and in compliance with applicable law, make a contribution to the Company in such amount as is required for the Company's Total Adjusted Capital to equal a percentage of its Company Action Level RBC determined to be appropriate by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In the event that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital for each of the Company's first and third fiscal quarters (as determined based on the Company's first and third fiscal quarterly filed statutory financial statements, respectively, subject to any adjustments or modifications thereto required by the Domiciliary State's insurance department or the Company's independent auditors) falls below the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's projected Company Action Level RBC (in each case as estimated by the Company as of the end of each such first and third fiscal quarters, as the case may be), AIG *may*, within the respective time periods set forth under paragraph 4, in accordance with paragraph 5 and in compliance with applicable law, provide to the Company cash, cash equivalents, securities or other instruments that qualify (as admitted assets) for purposes of calculating the Company's Total Adjusted Capital, as a contribution and not as a loan, in an amount such that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital as of the end of each of the Company's second and fourth fiscal quarter, as the case may be, will be projected to be at least equal to the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's Company Action Level RBC. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems necessary in its sole discretion and in compliance with applicable law, make a contribution to the Company in such amount as is required for the Company's Total Adjusted Capital to equal a percentage of its Company Action Level RBC determined to be appropriate by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' in Section 1 introduces ambiguity regarding AIG's obligation to provide capital. The original text mandates AIG to provide capital when certain conditions are met. The modification makes it discretionary. This contradicts the overall purpose of the agreement, which is to unconditionally maintain the Company's capital. This contradiction is directly evident in paragraph 9 where policyholders are given rights to enforce paragraphs 1, 4, and 5 of the agreement. If AIG's obligation is now discretionary, the policyholder's right to enforce becomes questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this 5 Agreement; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that no policyholder of the Company may take any action authorized under this paragraph 9 unless and until (a) such policyholder has given AIG written notice of its intent to enforce the terms of this Agreement as provided in this paragraph 9, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of and basis for the policyholder's complaint and (b) AIG has failed to comply with this Agreement within sixty (60) days after such notice is given; and, PROVIDED, FURTHER, that upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with paragraph 8 hereof, the rights of any policyholder as provided for under this paragraph 9 shall terminate effective as of the Termination Date, except with respect to the obligation of AIG (if any) to make capital contributions to the Company pursuant to paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement solely to the extent such obligation arose prior to, and remained unsatisfied as of, the Termination Date (it being understood that upon AIG's satisfaction of all such obligations after the Termination Date, no such policyholder shall have any rights against the Company or AIG, as the case may be, under this paragraph 9).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall' to 'may' in Section 1 fundamentally alters AIG's obligation to provide capital. Originally, it was mandatory under specific conditions; now, it is discretionary. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 9, which grants policyholders the right to enforce paragraphs 1, 4, and 5. The policyholders' rights to enforce paragraph 1 are undermined because the obligation has become discretionary for AIG. This ambiguity creates a legally significant contradiction within the contract, making it unclear whether AIG is obligated to act and whether policyholders have a right to enforce such action. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the key obligation regarding capital provision is now inconsistent and unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 3. For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital", "Company Action Level RBC", and "Surplus to Policyholders" shall have the meanings ascribed thereto under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State, or, with respect to "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC", if not defined therein, shall have the meanings ascribed thereto in the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC").
perturbation.changed_text: 3. For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital", "Company Action Level RBC", and "Surplus to Policyholders" shall have the meanings ascribed thereto as solely determined by AIG, irrespective of meanings ascribed under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State, or the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC").
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 3 to state that AIG solely determines the definitions of key financial terms creates a direct contradiction with the introductory 'WHEREAS' clauses, particularly the one stating the Company is subject to capital requirements of the insurance laws and regulations of Delaware. It creates an in-text contradiction where the agreement claims to adhere to regulatory capital requirements, while simultaneously granting AIG the unilateral power to define the metrics used to assess those requirements. This power undermines the regulatory oversight mentioned earlier. Specifically, the contradiction impacts Section 1, which references these defined terms in the context of AIG's capital contribution obligations. If AIG controls the definitions, they can manipulate the 'Total Adjusted Capital' or 'Company Action Level RBC' to avoid making contributions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In the event that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital for each of the Company's first and third fiscal quarters (as determined based on the Company's first and third fiscal quarterly filed statutory financial statements, respectively, subject to any adjustments or modifications thereto required by the Domiciliary State's insurance department or the Company's independent auditors) falls below the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's projected Company Action Level RBC (in each case as estimated by the Company as of the end of each such first and third fiscal quarters, as the case may be), AIG may, within the respective time periods set forth under paragraph 4, in accordance with paragraph 5 and in compliance with applicable law, provide to the Company cash, cash equivalents, securities or other instruments that qualify (as admitted assets) for purposes of calculating the Company's Total Adjusted Capital, as a contribution and not as a loan, in an amount such that the Company's Total Adjusted Capital as of the end of each of the Company's second and fourth fiscal quarter, as the case may be, will be projected to be at least equal to the Specified Minimum Percentage of the Company's Company Action Level RBC. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AIG may, at any time as it deems necessary in its sole discretion and in compliance with applicable law, make a contribution to the Company in such amount as is required for the Company's Total Adjusted Capital to equal a percentage of its Company Action Level RBC determined to be appropriate by the Company and AIG.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 gives AIG sole discretion in defining key financial terms, directly contradicting Section 1, which relies on these terms being determined by the domiciliary state's insurance department or the NAIC. By allowing AIG to unilaterally define "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC", the changed text undermines the regulatory oversight and capital contribution obligations outlined in Section 1, creating a significant in-text contradiction. This makes Section 1 unenforceable, as AIG could manipulate these definitions to avoid making contributions, violating the agreement's initial intention.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such sale. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with this paragraph 8, this Agreement shall continue indefinitely. AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless AIG *deems it necessary* to terminate this Agreement, the AIG can at any moment terminate the agreement upon 30 days prior written notice. To the extent not terminated previously by AIG pursuant to the foregoing, this Agreement will terminate automatically one year after the closing of any sale of the Company by AIG, and all provisions hereof will be of no further force and effect. For the avoidance of doubt, the termination of this Agreement pursuant to this paragraph 8 shall not relieve either party of any obligation it may owe to the other party hereunder that existed prior to, and remains outstanding as of, the Termination Date.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the conditions under which AIG can terminate the agreement in Section 8, replacing the objective criteria (significant corporate structure change, sale to a qualified acquirer) with a subjective 'AIG deems it necessary', the clause directly contradicts with itself. Previously, the PROVIDED HOWEVER listed specific scenarios. Now, it gives AIG unilateral power. Paragraph 9 allows policyholders to enforce paragraphs 1, 4, and 5 until termination. If AIG can terminate at will, the policyholders' rights become significantly weaker, creating uncertainty around those rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this 5 Agreement; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that no policyholder of the Company may take any action authorized under this paragraph 9 unless and until (a) such policyholder has given AIG written notice of its intent to enforce the terms of this Agreement as provided in this paragraph 9, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of and basis for the policyholder's complaint and (b) AIG has failed to comply with this Agreement within sixty (60) days after such notice is given; and, PROVIDED, FURTHER, that upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with paragraph 8 hereof, the rights of any policyholder as provided for under this paragraph 9 shall terminate effective as of the Termination Date, except with respect to the obligation of AIG (if any) to make capital contributions to the Company pursuant to paragraphs 1, 4 and 5 of this Agreement solely to the extent such obligation arose prior to, and remained unsatisfied as of, the Termination Date (it being understood that upon AIG's satisfaction of all such obligations after the Termination Date, no such policyholder shall have any rights against the Company or AIG, as the case may be, under this paragraph 9).
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 8 fundamentally alters AIG's termination rights, changing them from being contingent on specific, objective conditions to being based on AIG's subjective determination. This directly contradicts the policyholder's right to enforce paragraphs 1, 4, and 5 as outlined in Section 9. Section 9's enforceability is contingent on the termination conditions set forth in paragraph 8; therefore, the modification creates a strong in-text contradiction. With the 'AIG deems it necessary' clause, the policyholder's enforcement rights become uncertain and significantly weaker, creating uncertainty around those rights.
